# google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource

A PersistentResource that can be used to run custom workloads.


To get more information about PersistentResource, see:

* [API documentation](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.persistentResources)
* How-to Guides
    * [Official Documentation](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs)

<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=vertex_ai_persistent_resource_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Vertex Ai Persistent Resource Basic


```hcl
resource "google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource" "persistent_resource" {
  name = "persistent-resource"
  location = "us-central1"
  resource_pools {
    machine_spec {
      machine_type = "e2-standard-4"
    }
    replica_count = 1
    disk_spec {
      boot_disk_type = "pd-standard"
      boot_disk_size_gb = 100
    }
  }
}
```
## Example Usage - Vertex Ai Persistent Resource Full


```hcl
data "google_project" "project" {}

resource "google_kms_crypto_key_iam_member" "crypto_key" {
  crypto_key_id = "kms-name"
  role          = "roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter"
  member        = "serviceAccount:service-${data.google_project.project.number}@gcp-sa-aiplatform.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
}

resource "google_compute_network" "network" {
  name                    = "network-name"
  auto_create_subnetworks = false
}

resource "google_service_account" "service_account" {
  account_id   = "sa-name"
  display_name = "Service Account"
}

resource "google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource" "persistent_resource" {
  name = "persistent-resource"
  location = "us-central1"
  display_name = "example-resource"
  
  labels = {
    env = "test"
  }
  
  network = google_compute_network.network.id
  
  resource_pools {
    id = "head-node"
    machine_spec {
      machine_type = "n1-standard-4"
    }
    replica_count = 1
  }
  
  resource_pools {
    id = "worker-node"
    machine_spec {
      machine_type = "n1-standard-4"
    }
    replica_count = 1
    autoscaling_spec {
      min_replica_count = 1
      max_replica_count = 3
    }
  }

  resource_runtime_spec {
    ray_spec {
      head_node_resource_pool_id = "head-node"
    }
    service_account_spec {
      enable_custom_service_account = true
      service_account               = google_service_account.service_account.email
    }
  }

  encryption_spec {
    kms_key_name = "kms-name"
  }

  depends_on = [google_kms_crypto_key_iam_member.crypto_key]
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `name` -
  (Required)
  The name of the PersistentResource.

* `resource_pools` -
  (Required)
  Required. The spec of the pools of different resources.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_pools).

* `location` -
  (Required)
  The location for the resource


* `display_name` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. The display name of the PersistentResource. The name can be up to 128 characters long and can consist of any UTF-8 characters.

* `network` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. The full name of the Compute Engine network to peered with Vertex AI to host the persistent resources.

* `psc_interface_config` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Configuration for PSC-I for PersistentResource.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_psc_interface_config).

* `reserved_ip_ranges` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. A list of names for the reserved IP ranges under the VPC network that can be used for this persistent resource.

* `resource_runtime_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Persistent Resource runtime spec. For example, used for Ray cluster configuration.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_runtime_spec).

* `labels` -
  (Optional)
  The labels with user-defined metadata to organize PersistentResource.
  **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration.
  Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.

* `encryption_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Customer-managed encryption key spec for a PersistentResource.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_encryption_spec).

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_resource_pools"></a>The `resource_pools` block supports:

* `id` -
  (Optional)
  Immutable. The unique ID in a PersistentResource for referring to this resource pool. User can specify it if necessary. Otherwise, it's generated automatically.

* `machine_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Required. Immutable. The specification of a single machine.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_pools_machine_spec).

* `disk_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Disk spec for the machine in this node pool.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_pools_disk_spec).

* `replica_count` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. The total number of machines to use for this resource pool.

* `autoscaling_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Optional spec to configure GKE or Ray-on-Vertex autoscaling
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_pools_autoscaling_spec).


<a name="nested_resource_pools_machine_spec"></a>The `machine_spec` block supports:

* `machine_type` -
  (Optional)
  Immutable. The type of the machine.

* `accelerator_type` -
  (Optional)
  Immutable. The type of accelerator(s) that may be attached to the machine as per accelerator_count.

* `accelerator_count` -
  (Optional)
  The number of accelerators to attach to the machine.

* `gpu_partition_size` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Immutable. The Nvidia GPU partition size.

* `tpu_topology` -
  (Optional)
  Immutable. The topology of the TPUs. Corresponds to the TPU topologies available from GKE.

<a name="nested_resource_pools_disk_spec"></a>The `disk_spec` block supports:

* `boot_disk_type` -
  (Optional)
  Type of the boot disk.

* `boot_disk_size_gb` -
  (Optional)
  Size in GB of the boot disk (default is 100GB).

<a name="nested_resource_pools_autoscaling_spec"></a>The `autoscaling_spec` block supports:

* `min_replica_count` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Min replica count for autoscaling.

* `max_replica_count` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Max replica count for autoscaling.

<a name="nested_psc_interface_config"></a>The `psc_interface_config` block supports:

* `network_attachment` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. The name of the Compute Engine network attachment to attach to the resource.

* `dns_peering_configs` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. DNS peering configurations.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_psc_interface_config_dns_peering_configs).


<a name="nested_psc_interface_config_dns_peering_configs"></a>The `dns_peering_configs` block supports:

* `target_domain` -
  (Required)
  Required. The target domain for the DNS peering.

* `dns_name` -
  (Required)
  Required. The DNS name.

<a name="nested_resource_runtime_spec"></a>The `resource_runtime_spec` block supports:

* `ray_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Ray cluster configuration. Required when creating a dedicated RayCluster on the PersistentResource.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_runtime_spec_ray_spec).

* `service_account_spec` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Configure the use of workload identity on the PersistentResource
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_resource_runtime_spec_service_account_spec).


<a name="nested_resource_runtime_spec_ray_spec"></a>The `ray_spec` block supports:

* `image_uri` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Default image for user to choose a preferred ML framework.

* `resource_pool_images` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Required if image_uri isn't set. A map of resource_pool_id to prebuild Ray image.

* `head_node_resource_pool_id` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. This will be used to indicate which resource pool will serve as the Ray head node.

<a name="nested_resource_runtime_spec_service_account_spec"></a>The `service_account_spec` block supports:

* `enable_custom_service_account` -
  (Required)
  Required. If true, custom user-managed service account is enforced to run any workloads (for example, Vertex Jobs) on the resource.

* `service_account` -
  (Optional)
  Optional. Required when all below conditions are met * `enable_custom_service_account` is true; * any runtime is specified via `ResourceRuntimeSpec` on creation time, for example, Ray.

<a name="nested_encryption_spec"></a>The `encryption_spec` block supports:

* `kms_key_name` -
  (Required)
  Required. The Cloud KMS resource identifier of the customer managed encryption key used to protect a resource.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/persistentResources/{{name}}`

* `state` -
  Output only. The detailed state of a Study.

* `error` -
  Output only. Only populated when persistent resource's state is STOPPING or ERROR.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_error).

* `create_time` -
  Output only. Time when the PersistentResource was created.

* `start_time` -
  Output only. Time when the PersistentResource for the first time entered the RUNNING state.

* `update_time` -
  Output only. Time when the PersistentResource was most recently updated.

* `terraform_labels` -
  The combination of labels configured directly on the resource
   and default labels configured on the provider.

* `effective_labels` -
  All of labels (key/value pairs) present on the resource in GCP, including the labels configured through Terraform, other clients and services.


<a name="nested_error"></a>The `error` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)

* `message` -
  (Output)

## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 60 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 60 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 60 minutes.

## Import


PersistentResource can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/persistentResources/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{name}}`
* `{{location}}/{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import PersistentResource using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-required value->"
    location = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import PersistentResource using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/persistentResources/{{name}}"
  to = google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), PersistentResource can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/persistentResources/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_vertex_ai_persistent_resource.default {{location}}/{{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
